overview: This insect is a Devil's Flower Mantis (Idolomantis diabolica), one of the largest flower-mimicking mantises in the world. It is native to East African countries including Tanzania, Ethiopia, and Kenya. They are known for their vibrant colors and ability to mimic flowers to ambush prey. While not officially endangered, they face threats from habitat loss and climate change. Wikipedia +4 5 sites Idolomantis - Wikipedia

This insect is a Devil's Flower Mantis (Idolomantis diabolica), one of the largest flower-mimicking mantises in the world.
It is native to East African countries including Tanzania, Ethiopia, and Kenya.
They are known for their vibrant colors and ability to mimic flowers to ambush prey.
While not officially endangered, they face threats from habitat loss and climate change.
